a_image.alt: Mac OS Classic UI style mood board — Apple Design · 1984–2001

sections.a_description: The monochrome elegance of the original Macintosh — platinum gray surfaces, pixel-perfect 1-bit precision, horizontal pinstripe title bars, and the beautiful simplicity of an interface drawn entirely in black, white, and gray. Mac OS Classic UI captures the era when Apple's design language was defined by Susan Kare's icons and the constraints of a 512x342 pixel screen.

Mac OS Classic styling appears on retro computing museums, Apple history archives, and nostalgic developer portfolios. It's popular with designers who appreciate the discipline of monochrome constraints, showing up on minimalist personal sites, typography experiments, and creative projects that find beauty in the austere precision of early desktop publishing.

a_image_cover.alt: Mac OS Classic UI style use case illustration
Mac OS Classic UI style use case illustration
Mac OS Classic UI style use case illustration

sections.a_description: The pre-OS X Macintosh interface — clean white/platinum gray surfaces, black-and-white pixel precision, the Chicago font era, and those iconic horizontal pinstripes. Background is platinum gray (#eeeeee). The .inner panel emulates a classic Mac window: white background with a 1px solid black border, a title bar with fine horizontal stripes (1px alternating black/white pattern via repeating-linear-gradient). The aesthetic is monochrome with surgical precision — no gradients, no color splash, just black (#000000), white (#ffffff), and shades of gray. Zero border-radius. Set in Inter for body text — its clean, neutral geometry channels the Geneva/Chicago system font spirit. Use Courier Prime for the eyebrow — the monospaced type adds a technical, early-desktop feel. Heading in Inter at weight 700, 28px, line-height 1.2, letter-spacing -0.5px, black. Eyebrow in Courier Prime at 12px, weight 400, uppercase, 2px letter-spacing, color #555. Body in Inter at weight 400, 14px, line-height 1.6, color #222. Buttons: black background, white text, 0 border-radius, 1px solid black border. On hover: inverted (white background, black text). Secondary: white background, black border, black text; hover: black background, white text. Classic Mac UI inverted on click. Alternatives: IBM Plex Sans (system UI feel), Roboto (neutral), SF Mono (Apple monospace).
